summary: Code defines behavior and configuration defines values: any value that can differ between deployments, environments, operators, or served groups lives in a declared resolution layer, never hardcoded.

rules.rule: Hardcode a value that can differ between deployments, environments, operators, or served groups — credentials, service URLs, user-visible text, visual identity, feature flags, limits, or the active provider; a hardcoded configurable value is an architectural bug, not a shortcut.

watch.note: declared unwatched — no gate cites this canon yet; growth candidate

rules.rule: Resolve every configurable value through the declared layers most-specific-first (per-group store → platform defaults → environment variables → code default), with code defaults reserved for non-sensitive, non-varying values only.

watch.note: declared unwatched — no gate cites this canon yet; growth candidate

rules.rule: Let a secret reach the code-default layer or invent a default when a secret is absent — the feature fails closed instead.

watch.note: declared unwatched — no gate cites this canon yet; growth candidate

rules.rule: Before declaring that a key, secret, or config value 'does not exist', follow the same resolution path the code uses at runtime — read the module's resolver and query the same stores it queries before asking the human.

watch.note: declared unwatched — no gate cites this canon yet; growth candidate

rules.rule: Run the one-question hardcode test on any diff before commit — 'if a different deployment/group/operator needed a different value here, would the code have to change?' — and if yes, move the value to the right layer.

watch.note: declared unwatched — no gate cites this canon yet; growth candidate
